Go-to-Market Strategy
Retrofit-Recommender: AI-Enabled Industrial Sustainability Platform
Version: 1.0
Date: January 29, 2026
Owner: Product & Sales Leadership
Executive Summary
Retrofit-Recommender targets the $12B industrial maintenance software market with a differentiated AI-sustainability positioning. Our go-to-market strategy combines product-led growth (freemium tier for awareness) with enterprise direct sales (high-touch for $50K+ deals) and partner channels (sustainability consultancies as resellers).
12-Month Goal: $2M ARR, 50+ paying customers, 10,000 tons CO2e avoided
Unique Value Proposition: The only AI platform that connects equipment retrofit decisions to quantified sustainability outcomes (CO2e, compliance frameworks) with instant business case generation—reducing decision cycles from 3 months to 3 days.
Market Opportunity
Total Addressable Market (TAM)
$12B — Global industrial asset management software market (Gartner, 2025)
Segments:
EAM (Enterprise Asset Management): $6.5B
Predictive Maintenance: $3.2B
Sustainability Software: $2.3B
Serviceable Addressable Market (SAM)
$1.2B — Industrial facilities (500+ assets) in regulated markets (North America, EU) with sustainability commitments
Target Industries:
Manufacturing (35% of SAM): Chemical, automotive, electronics, food processing
Energy & Utilities (30%): Power generation, water treatment, oil & gas
Commercial Real Estate (20%): Large facilities (>500K sq ft) with energy management
Heavy Industry (15%): Steel, mining, pulp & paper
Serviceable Obtainable Market (SOM)
$60M — 5% market share in 3 years (realistic for focused startup)
Assumptions:
1,000 target companies (Fortune 2000 + high-growth industrials)
5% penetration (50 customers) @ $40K ARPU = $2M ARR (Year 1)
15% penetration (150 customers) @ $60K ARPU = $9M ARR (Year 2)
25% penetration (250 customers) @ $80K ARPU = $20M ARR (Year 3)
Competitive Landscape
Direct Competitors
Competitor
Strengths
Weaknesses
Our Advantage
SAP Asset Intelligence Network
Enterprise scale, ERP integration
No sustainability focus, generic AI, complex implementation (9-18 months)
Purpose-built for sustainability, 10x faster deployment (1-2 weeks)
IBM Maximo + AI
Mature platform, strong in utilities
Limited retrofit recommendations, expensive ($100K+ annually)
Retrofit-specific, 50% lower cost, explainable AI
Siemens Industrial Edge
Deep OT expertise, IoT integration
Siemens hardware lock-in, no vendor-neutral recommendations
Vendor-neutral, 3x catalog breadth
Augury (Predictive Maintenance)
Real-time monitoring, failure prediction
Tells "when" but not "what to do", no sustainability ROI
Actionable recommendations + CO2e impact
Indirect Competitors
Sustainability consultants (Deloitte, EY, local firms): High-touch, expensive ($150-$300/hr), not scalable → We automate 80% of their work
Manual processes (Excel, tribal knowledge): Free but error-prone, slow, no audit trail → We provide instant, accurate, auditable analysis
Competitive Moats (Defensibility)
Data Network Effect: Every implementation improves recommendation accuracy (outcome feedback loop)
Domain Expertise: Proprietary knowledge base built from 100+ interviews + industry best practices
Sustainability IP: Patent-pending lifecycle carbon calculation methodology
Ecosystem Integrations: First-mover advantage with CMMS (Maximo, SAP) and carbon platforms (Watershed, Persefoni)
Target Customer Segmentation
Tier 1: Enterprise (>2,000 employees, 1,000+ assets)
Characteristics:
Annual maintenance budget: $5M-$50M
Sustainability commitments (SBTi, Net Zero 2050)
Multiple facilities (5-50 sites)
IT infrastructure for integration (SAP, Maximo)
Buying Process:
Decision-maker: VP Operations OR Chief Sustainability Officer
Influencers: Facilities managers, procurement, IT, finance
Buying Cycle: 6-9 months (pilot → approval → rollout)
ACV (Annual Contract Value): $80K-$150K
Examples: Dow Chemical, Toyota, Johnson & Johnson, ArcelorMittal
GTM Motion: Direct sales with executive sponsorship
Tier 2: Mid-Market (500-2,000 employees, 200-1,000 assets)
Characteristics:
Annual maintenance budget: $1M-$5M
Emerging sustainability programs
1-5 facilities
Basic IT (may lack ERP)
Buying Process:
Decision-maker: Plant Manager OR Facilities Director
Influencers: Maintenance engineers, energy manager
Buying Cycle: 3-6 months
ACV: $30K-$60K
Examples: Regional manufacturers, mid-size food processors, industrial parks
GTM Motion: Inside sales + product-led growth (PLG)
Tier 3: SMB (<500 employees, <200 assets)
Characteristics:
Annual maintenance budget: <$1M
Limited or no sustainability reporting
Single facility
No ERP system
Buying Process:
Decision-maker: Plant Manager (hands-on technical role)
Buying Cycle: 1-3 months
ACV: $5K-$15K
GTM Motion: Product-led growth (freemium → self-serve upgrade)
Pricing Strategy
Tier 1: Freemium (SMB Acquisition)
Price: $0/month
Limits:
10 recommendations per month
Basic catalog (30 products)
No API access
Community support only
Goal: Generate 500+ freemium users for brand awareness, funnel to paid tiers
Tier 2: Professional (Mid-Market)
Price: $2,500/month ($30K annually)
Includes:
100 recommendations per month
Full catalog (250+ products)
CO2e tracking dashboard
Email support (48-hour SLA)
1 user seat (additional seats +$500/mo)
Target: 30 customers by Q4 2026 = $900K ARR
Tier 3: Enterprise (Large Customers)
Price: $50K-$150K annually (negotiated)
Includes:
Unlimited recommendations
API access (CMMS/ERP integration)
Custom catalog integration
Dedicated Customer Success Manager
Priority support (4-hour SLA)
White-label option
Multi-facility deployment
SLA guarantee (99.5% uptime)
Target: 10-15 customers by Q4 2026 = $750K-$1.5M ARR
Add-On Services
Implementation: $25K (one-time) — Custom knowledge base, API setup, training
Training: $5K per day — On-site workshops for maintenance teams
Custom Catalog: $15K — Integrate customer's preferred vendors/products
Annual Strategic Review: $10K — Executive-level sustainability impact assessment
Sales & Distribution Channels
Channel 1: Direct Sales (Enterprise)
Target: 10-15 enterprise customers (50%+ of revenue)
Team:
1 VP Sales (Q2 hire)
2 Account Executives (Q3 hires)
1 Sales Engineer (Q4 hire)
Playbook:
Outbound: Target Fortune 1000 with public SBTi commitments
Demo: Executive-level presentation (30 min) showing ROI + CO2e impact
Pilot: 90-day proof-of-value with 1 facility ($10K pilot fee, credited to annual contract)
Expansion: Land-and-expand to additional facilities
Sales Cycle: 6-9 months
Close Rate: 15-20% (demo → pilot → close)
Q1 Actions:
Build target account list (200 companies)
Develop sales deck + ROI calculator
Hire VP Sales
Channel 2: Inside Sales (Mid-Market)
Target: 30-40 mid-market customers (30% of revenue)
Team:
1 Inside Sales Manager (Q3 hire)
2 SDRs (Sales Development Reps) (Q4 hires)
Playbook:
Inbound: Leads from content marketing, webinars, trade shows
Outbound: Cold outreach to regional manufacturers
Demo: Self-service demo video + live Q&A (30 min)
Trial: 30-day free trial (full Professional tier access)
Close: Credit card sign-up (no procurement process)
Sales Cycle: 2-4 months
Close Rate: 25-30%
Q2 Actions:
Build inbound lead gen engine (SEO, content)
Create self-service demo environment
Hire Inside Sales Manager
Channel 3: Product-Led Growth (PLG) (SMB)
Target: 100+ SMB customers (20% of revenue)
Strategy:
Freemium tier drives sign-ups (viral growth)
In-app upgrade prompts when hitting limits
Email nurture campaigns (case studies, webinars)
Conversion Funnel:
1,000 freemium sign-ups
10% activate (use product 5+ times) = 100 active users
20% convert to paid = 20 paying customers @ $30K = $600K ARR
Q2 Actions:
Launch freemium tier
Implement in-app onboarding flow
Build email nurture sequences
Channel 4: Partner Resellers (Consultancies)
Target: 5 partners, 10-15 customer referrals (10% of revenue)
Ideal Partners:
Sustainability consultancies (e.g., local EY/Deloitte offices)
Energy efficiency firms (e.g., ESCO partners)
Industrial engineering firms
Value Proposition to Partners:
Revenue Share: 30% of contract value
White-Label: Rebrand as partner's tool
Enablement: Training, co-marketing, lead sharing
Partner Playbook:
Consultants use Retrofit-Recommender during client engagements
Client sees value, wants ongoing access
Partner sells annual subscription, keeps 30% margin
Q3 Actions:
Recruit 3 pilot partners
Build partner portal (usage tracking, revenue reports)
Create co-branded marketing materials
Marketing Strategy
Positioning Statement
For industrial facilities with sustainability commitments,
Retrofit-Recommender is an AI-powered platform
That transforms equipment retrofit decisions into quantified sustainability outcomes.
Unlike generic EAM software or expensive consultants,
Our product delivers instant, data-driven recommendations with CO2e impact and business case generation—reducing decision cycles from months to days.
Messaging Framework
Headline: Accelerate Industrial Decarbonization with AI-Powered Retrofit Recommendations
Key Messages:
Speed: Reduce retrofit decision time from 3 months to 3 days
Impact: Quantify CO2e reductions for sustainability reporting (CDP, TCFD)
ROI: Generate instant business cases with TCO, payback, and savings projections
Trust: Vendor-neutral recommendations backed by explainable AI
Safety: Built-in validation prevents costly compatibility errors
Proof Points:
"12 pilot customers avoided 2,000+ tons CO2e in first year"
"95% recommendation accuracy validated by user feedback"
"Average payback period: 2.3 years"
Content Marketing (Inbound Lead Gen)
Blog/SEO (Target: 5,000 monthly organic visits by Q4)
Topics:
"How to Calculate Scope 2 Emissions from Equipment Upgrades" (Sustainability Directors)
"Valve Efficiency: 15% Energy Savings Guide" (Facilities Managers)
"Total Cost of Ownership: Industrial Equipment Calculator" (Procurement)
"AI in Maintenance: Complete Guide" (Technical audience)
Cadence: 2 posts per week (Q2+)
Case Studies (3 by Q3)
Format: Problem → Solution → Quantified Outcomes
Example:
Chemical Manufacturer Reduces CO2e by 1,200 Tons Annually with AI-Powered Retrofits
Challenge: 500+ aging valves, no visibility into efficiency
Solution: Retrofit-Recommender identified top 50 retrofit opportunities
Results: $180K annual savings, 1,200 tons CO2e avoided, 2.1-year payback
Distribution: Website, sales collateral, PR, LinkedIn
Webinars (Monthly, starting Q2)
Topics:
"AI for Industrial Sustainability: Getting Started"
"How to Build a Data-Driven Decarbonization Plan"
"Retrofit ROI: Financial Justification Workshop"
Goal: 100+ attendees per webinar, 20% conversion to trial
Thought Leadership
Speaking: Present at ACEEE, Industrial Energy Technology Conference, CDP Forums
Whitepapers: "The State of Industrial Decarbonization 2026" (research report)
Podcast: Guest appearances on sustainability/manufacturing podcasts
Paid Marketing (Later Stage, Q3+)
LinkedIn Ads (B2B focus)
Target: Sustainability Directors, VPs Operations, Facilities Managers at Fortune 2000
Budget: $10K/month
Goal: 500 MQLs (Marketing Qualified Leads) per quarter
Google Ads (Intent-based)
Keywords: "equipment retrofit calculator", "industrial CO2 reduction", "maintenance ROI tool"
Budget: $5K/month
Goal: 200 freemium sign-ups per month
Events & Trade Shows
Tier 1 Events (Must Attend)
ACEEE Summer Study (August): Energy efficiency focus
Industrial Energy Technology Conference (May): Maintenance + sustainability
Greenbuild (November): Commercial buildings
Goal: 50 qualified leads per event, 3 pilot customers
Tier 2 Events (Sponsor/Attend)
Regional manufacturing associations
Industry-specific (Food Processing Expo, Chem Show)
Customer Acquisition Strategy
Phase 1: Design Partners (Q1 2026) ✅
Goal: 3 design partners for product validation
Criteria:
Willing to provide feedback weekly
500+ assets
Sustainability commitment (SBTi or similar)
Pilot fee: $5K (discounted from $10K)
Status: 2 signed, 1 in negotiation
Phase 2: Early Adopters (Q2 2026)
Goal: 10 paying customers
Targeting:
Expand from design partner referrals
Outbound to companies with recent sustainability announcements
Target industries: Chemical, automotive, food processing
Offer: 50% discount for first year ($15K vs. $30K) in exchange for case study participation
Phase 3: Scale (Q3-Q4 2026)
Goal: 50 paying customers
Tactics:
Launch freemium tier (PLG motion)
Ramp up direct sales team
Partner channel activates
Content marketing generates inbound leads
Success Metrics (12-Month)
Revenue Metrics
ARR: $2M
MRR Growth: 15% month-over-month (Q2-Q4)
Avg Deal Size: $40K
Customer Count: 50 paying customers
Efficiency Metrics
CAC (Customer Acquisition Cost): <$5K blended
LTV:CAC Ratio: >5:1
Sales Cycle: <6 months (enterprise), <3 months (mid-market)
Win Rate: >20% (demo to close)
Product Metrics
Freemium Sign-ups: 1,000+
Free-to-Paid Conversion: 10%
NPS: >45
Logo Retention: >90%
Impact Metrics
CO2e Avoided: 10,000 tons (cumulative across customers)
Recommendations Generated: 5,000+
Implementation Rate: 25%
Launch Plan (Q2 2026)
Pre-Launch (Weeks 1-4)
Finalize pricing & packaging
Build sales deck + demo environment
Create 3 case studies (from design partners)
Launch website v2.0 (professional branding)
Set up marketing automation (HubSpot/Salesforce)
Launch Week (Week 5)
Press release + industry media outreach
LinkedIn announcement (founder + company page)
Email to 500+ waitlist contacts
Launch webinar: "Introducing Retrofit-Recommender"
Activate freemium tier
Post-Launch (Weeks 6-12)
Weekly sales pipeline review
Bi-weekly marketing performance review
Monthly NPS survey
Quarterly business review with customers
Budget (12-Month)
Personnel ($800K)
VP Sales: $180K + $90K OTE
2 AEs: $120K + $60K OTE each
Inside Sales Manager: $90K + $45K OTE
2 SDRs: $60K + $30K OTE each
Customer Success: $80K
Marketing ($250K)
Content/SEO: $50K
Events/Trade Shows: $75K
Paid Ads: $60K
PR/Agency: $40K
Tools (HubSpot, etc.): $25K
Tools & Infrastructure ($100K)
CRM (Salesforce): $15K
Marketing Automation: $12K
Analytics: $10K
Sales Enablement (Gong, Outreach): $20K
Cloud Infrastructure: $30K
Partnerships (Ecoinvent, APIs): $13K
Total GTM Budget: $1.15M (Year 1)
Expected Return: $2M ARR → 74% spend efficiency (acceptable for Year 1)
Risks & Mitigation
Risk
Probability
Impact
Mitigation
Slow enterprise sales cycles
High
High
Focus on PLG + mid-market for faster revenue
Low free-to-paid conversion (<5%)
Medium
Medium
A/B test onboarding, add usage-based triggers
Competitors copy positioning
Medium
Medium
Build data moat, deepen integrations, patent IP
Sustainability budget cuts (recession)
Low
High
Emphasize cost savings + ROI, not just carbon
Pilot customers don't renew
Medium
High
Proactive CS engagement, monthly business reviews
Next Steps (Q1 2026)
Week 1-2:
Finalize pricing (Board approval)
Hire VP Sales (kickoff search)
Launch content marketing (first 5 blog posts)
Week 3-4:
Design partner check-ins (feedback on new features)
Build target account list (200 companies)
Create sales enablement materials
Week 5-8:
Pilot freemium tier (50 beta users)
First webinar (Feb 15)
Submit conference proposals (ACEEE, IET)
Week 9-12:
Official freemium launch
VP Sales onboarding
First enterprise deal closed (target: March 31)
Document Control
Version: 1.0
Owner: VP Product + VP Sales
Review: Monthly
Next Update: March 1, 2026 (post-Q1 review)

Target: Sustainability Directors, VPs Operations, Facilities Managers at Fortune 2000

Google Ads (Intent-based)
Keywords: "equipment retrofit calculator", "industrial CO2 reduction", "maintenance ROI tool"
Budget: $5K/month
Goal: 200 freemium sign-ups per month
Events & Trade Shows
Tier 1 Events (Must Attend)
ACEEE Summer Study (August): Energy efficiency focus
Industrial Energy Technology Conference (May): Maintenance + sustainability
Greenbuild (November): Commercial buildings
Goal: 50 qualified leads per event, 3 pilot customers
Tier 2 Events (Sponsor/Attend)
Regional manufacturing associations
Industry-specific (Food Processing Expo, Chem Show)
Customer Acquisition Strategy
Phase 1: Design Partners (Q1 2026) ✅
Goal: 3 design partners for product validation
Criteria:
Willing to provide feedback weekly
500+ assets
Sustainability commitment (SBTi or similar)
Pilot fee: $5K (discounted from $10K)
Status: 2 signed, 1 in negotiation
Phase 2: Early Adopters (Q2 2026)
Goal: 10 paying customers
Targeting:
Expand from design partner referrals
Outbound to companies with recent sustainability announcements
Target industries: Chemical, automotive, food processing
Offer: 50% discount for first year ($15K vs. $30K) in exchange for case study participation
Phase 3: Scale (Q3-Q4 2026)
Goal: 50 paying customers
Tactics:
Launch freemium tier (PLG motion)
Ramp up direct sales team
Partner channel activates
Content marketing generates inbound leads
Success Metrics (12-Month)
Revenue Metrics
ARR: $2M
MRR Growth: 15% month-over-month (Q2-Q4)
Avg Deal Size: $40K
Customer Count: 50 paying customers
Efficiency Metrics
CAC (Customer Acquisition Cost): <$5K blended
LTV:CAC Ratio: >5:1
Sales Cycle: <6 months (enterprise), <3 months (mid-market)
Win Rate: >20% (demo to close)
Product Metrics
Freemium Sign-ups: 1,000+
Free-to-Paid Conversion: 10%
NPS: >45
Logo Retention: >90%
Impact Metrics
CO2e Avoided: 10,000 tons (cumulative across customers)
Recommendations Generated: 5,000+
Implementation Rate: 25%
Launch Plan (Q2 2026)
Pre-Launch (Weeks 1-4)
Finalize pricing & packaging
Build sales deck + demo environment
Create 3 case studies (from design partners)
Launch website v2.0 (professional branding)
Set up marketing automation (HubSpot/Salesforce)
Launch Week (Week 5)
Press release + industry media outreach
LinkedIn announcement (founder + company page)
Email to 500+ waitlist contacts
Launch webinar: "Introducing Retrofit-Recommender"
Activate freemium tier
Post-Launch (Weeks 6-12)
Weekly sales pipeline review
Bi-weekly marketing performance review
Monthly NPS survey
Quarterly business review with customers
Budget (12-Month)
Personnel ($800K)
VP Sales: $180K + $90K OTE
2 AEs: $120K + $60K OTE each
Inside Sales Manager: $90K + $45K OTE
2 SDRs: $60K + $30K OTE each
Customer Success: $80K
Marketing ($250K)
Content/SEO: $50K
Events/Trade Shows: $75K
Paid Ads: $60K
PR/Agency: $40K
Tools (HubSpot, etc.): $25K
Tools & Infrastructure ($100K)
CRM (Salesforce): $15K
Marketing Automation: $12K
Analytics: $10K
Sales Enablement (Gong, Outreach): $20K
Cloud Infrastructure: $30K
Partnerships (Ecoinvent, APIs): $13K
Total GTM Budget: $1.15M (Year 1)
Expected Return: $2M ARR → 74% spend efficiency (acceptable for Year 1)
Risks & Mitigation
Risk
Probability
Impact
Mitigation
Slow enterprise sales cycles
High
High
Focus on PLG + mid-market for faster revenue
Low free-to-paid conversion (<5%)
Medium
Medium
A/B test onboarding, add usage-based triggers
Competitors copy positioning
Medium
Medium
Build data moat, deepen integrations, patent IP
Sustainability budget cuts (recession)
Low
High
Emphasize cost savings + ROI, not just carbon
Pilot customers don't renew
Medium
High
Proactive CS engagement, monthly business reviews
Next Steps (Q1 2026)

Finalize pricing (Board approval)
Hire VP Sales (kickoff search)
Launch content marketing (first 5 blog posts)

Design partner check-ins (feedback on new features)
Build target account list (200 companies)

VP Sales onboarding

Owner: VP Product + VP Sales
Review: Monthly

Go-to-Market Strategy
Retrofit-Recommender: AI-Enabled Industrial Sustainability Platform
Version: 1.0
Date: January 29, 2026
Owner: Product & Sales Leadership
Executive Summary
Retrofit-Recommender targets the $12B industrial maintenance software market with a differentiated AI-sustainability positioning. Our go-to-market strategy combines product-led growth (freemium tier for awareness) with enterprise direct sales (high-touch for $50K+ deals) and partner channels (sustainability consultancies as resellers).
12-Month Goal: $2M ARR, 50+ paying customers, 10,000 tons CO2e avoided
Unique Value Proposition: The only AI platform that connects equipment retrofit decisions to quantified sustainability outcomes (CO2e, compliance frameworks) with instant business case generation—reducing decision cycles from 3 months to 3 days.
Market Opportunity
Total Addressable Market (TAM)
$12B — Global industrial asset management software market (Gartner, 2025)
Segments:
EAM (Enterprise Asset Management): $6.5B
Predictive Maintenance: $3.2B
Sustainability Software: $2.3B
Serviceable Addressable Market (SAM)
$1.2B — Industrial facilities (500+ assets) in regulated markets (North America, EU) with sustainability commitments
Target Industries:
Manufacturing (35% of SAM): Chemical, automotive, electronics, food processing
Energy & Utilities (30%): Power generation, water treatment, oil & gas
Commercial Real Estate (20%): Large facilities (>500K sq ft) with energy management
Heavy Industry (15%): Steel, mining, pulp & paper
Serviceable Obtainable Market (SOM)
$60M — 5% market share in 3 years (realistic for focused startup)
Assumptions:
1,000 target companies (Fortune 2000 + high-growth industrials)
5% penetration (50 customers) @ $40K ARPU = $2M ARR (Year 1)
15% penetration (150 customers) @ $60K ARPU = $9M ARR (Year 2)
25% penetration (250 customers) @ $80K ARPU = $20M ARR (Year 3)
Competitive Landscape
Direct Competitors
Competitor
Strengths
Weaknesses
Our Advantage
SAP Asset Intelligence Network
Enterprise scale, ERP integration
No sustainability focus, generic AI, complex implementation (9-18 months)
Purpose-built for sustainability, 10x faster deployment (1-2 weeks)
IBM Maximo + AI
Mature platform, strong in utilities
Limited retrofit recommendations, expensive ($100K+ annually)
Retrofit-specific, 50% lower cost, explainable AI
Siemens Industrial Edge
Deep OT expertise, IoT integration
Siemens hardware lock-in, no vendor-neutral recommendations
Vendor-neutral, 3x catalog breadth
Augury (Predictive Maintenance)
Real-time monitoring, failure prediction
Tells "when" but not "what to do", no sustainability ROI
Actionable recommendations + CO2e impact
Indirect Competitors
Sustainability consultants (Deloitte, EY, local firms): High-touch, expensive ($150-$300/hr), not scalable → We automate 80% of their work
Manual processes (Excel, tribal knowledge): Free but error-prone, slow, no audit trail → We provide instant, accurate, auditable analysis
Competitive Moats (Defensibility)
Data Network Effect: Every implementation improves recommendation accuracy (outcome feedback loop)
Domain Expertise: Proprietary knowledge base built from 100+ interviews + industry best practices
Sustainability IP: Patent-pending lifecycle carbon calculation methodology
Ecosystem Integrations: First-mover advantage with CMMS (Maximo, SAP) and carbon platforms (Watershed, Persefoni)
Target Customer Segmentation
Tier 1: Enterprise (>2,000 employees, 1,000+ assets)
Characteristics:
Annual maintenance budget: $5M-$50M
Sustainability commitments (SBTi, Net Zero 2050)
Multiple facilities (5-50 sites)
IT infrastructure for integration (SAP, Maximo)
Buying Process:
Decision-maker: VP Operations OR Chief Sustainability Officer
Influencers: Facilities managers, procurement, IT, finance
Buying Cycle: 6-9 months (pilot → approval → rollout)
ACV (Annual Contract Value): $80K-$150K
Examples: Dow Chemical, Toyota, Johnson & Johnson, ArcelorMittal
GTM Motion: Direct sales with executive sponsorship
Tier 2: Mid-Market (500-2,000 employees, 200-1,000 assets)
Characteristics:
Annual maintenance budget: $1M-$5M
Emerging sustainability programs
1-5 facilities
Basic IT (may lack ERP)
Buying Process:
Decision-maker: Plant Manager OR Facilities Director
Influencers: Maintenance engineers, energy manager
Buying Cycle: 3-6 months
ACV: $30K-$60K
Examples: Regional manufacturers, mid-size food processors, industrial parks
GTM Motion: Inside sales + product-led growth (PLG)
Tier 3: SMB (<500 employees, <200 assets)
Characteristics:
Annual maintenance budget: <$1M
Limited or no sustainability reporting
Single facility
No ERP system
Buying Process:
Decision-maker: Plant Manager (hands-on technical role)
Buying Cycle: 1-3 months
ACV: $5K-$15K
GTM Motion: Product-led growth (freemium → self-serve upgrade)
Pricing Strategy
Tier 1: Freemium (SMB Acquisition)
Price: $0/month
Limits:
10 recommendations per month
Basic catalog (30 products)
No API access
Community support only
Goal: Generate 500+ freemium users for brand awareness, funnel to paid tiers
Tier 2: Professional (Mid-Market)
Price: $2,500/month ($30K annually)
Includes:
100 recommendations per month
Full catalog (250+ products)
CO2e tracking dashboard
Email support (48-hour SLA)
1 user seat (additional seats +$500/mo)
Target: 30 customers by Q4 2026 = $900K ARR
Tier 3: Enterprise (Large Customers)
Price: $50K-$150K annually (negotiated)
Includes:
Unlimited recommendations
API access (CMMS/ERP integration)
Custom catalog integration
Dedicated Customer Success Manager
Priority support (4-hour SLA)
White-label option
Multi-facility deployment
SLA guarantee (99.5% uptime)
Target: 10-15 customers by Q4 2026 = $750K-$1.5M ARR
Add-On Services
Implementation: $25K (one-time) — Custom knowledge base, API setup, training
Training: $5K per day — On-site workshops for maintenance teams
Custom Catalog: $15K — Integrate customer's preferred vendors/products
Annual Strategic Review: $10K — Executive-level sustainability impact assessment
Sales & Distribution Channels
Channel 1: Direct Sales (Enterprise)
Target: 10-15 enterprise customers (50%+ of revenue)
Team:
1 VP Sales (Q2 hire)
2 Account Executives (Q3 hires)
1 Sales Engineer (Q4 hire)
Playbook:
Outbound: Target Fortune 1000 with public SBTi commitments
Demo: Executive-level presentation (30 min) showing ROI + CO2e impact
Pilot: 90-day proof-of-value with 1 facility ($10K pilot fee, credited to annual contract)
Expansion: Land-and-expand to additional facilities
Sales Cycle: 6-9 months
Close Rate: 15-20% (demo → pilot → close)
Q1 Actions:
Build target account list (200 companies)
Develop sales deck + ROI calculator
Hire VP Sales
Channel 2: Inside Sales (Mid-Market)
Target: 30-40 mid-market customers (30% of revenue)
Team:
1 Inside Sales Manager (Q3 hire)
2 SDRs (Sales Development Reps) (Q4 hires)
Playbook:
Inbound: Leads from content marketing, webinars, trade shows
Outbound: Cold outreach to regional manufacturers
Demo: Self-service demo video + live Q&A (30 min)
Trial: 30-day free trial (full Professional tier access)
Close: Credit card sign-up (no procurement process)
Sales Cycle: 2-4 months
Close Rate: 25-30%
Q2 Actions:
Build inbound lead gen engine (SEO, content)
Create self-service demo environment
Hire Inside Sales Manager
Channel 3: Product-Led Growth (PLG) (SMB)
Target: 100+ SMB customers (20% of revenue)
Strategy:
Freemium tier drives sign-ups (viral growth)
In-app upgrade prompts when hitting limits
Email nurture campaigns (case studies, webinars)
Conversion Funnel:
1,000 freemium sign-ups
10% activate (use product 5+ times) = 100 active users
20% convert to paid = 20 paying customers @ $30K = $600K ARR
Q2 Actions:
Launch freemium tier
Implement in-app onboarding flow
Build email nurture sequences
Channel 4: Partner Resellers (Consultancies)
Target: 5 partners, 10-15 customer referrals (10% of revenue)
Ideal Partners:
Sustainability consultancies (e.g., local EY/Deloitte offices)
Energy efficiency firms (e.g., ESCO partners)
Industrial engineering firms
Value Proposition to Partners:
Revenue Share: 30% of contract value
White-Label: Rebrand as partner's tool
Enablement: Training, co-marketing, lead sharing
Partner Playbook:
Consultants use Retrofit-Recommender during client engagements
Client sees value, wants ongoing access
Partner sells annual subscription, keeps 30% margin
Q3 Actions:
Recruit 3 pilot partners
Build partner portal (usage tracking, revenue reports)
Create co-branded marketing materials
Marketing Strategy
Positioning Statement
For industrial facilities with sustainability commitments,
Retrofit-Recommender is an AI-powered platform
That transforms equipment retrofit decisions into quantified sustainability outcomes.
Unlike generic EAM software or expensive consultants,
Our product delivers instant, data-driven recommendations with CO2e impact and business case generation—reducing decision cycles from months to days.
Messaging Framework
Headline: Accelerate Industrial Decarbonization with AI-Powered Retrofit Recommendations
Key Messages:
Speed: Reduce retrofit decision time from 3 months to 3 days
Impact: Quantify CO2e reductions for sustainability reporting (CDP, TCFD)
ROI: Generate instant business cases with TCO, payback, and savings projections
Trust: Vendor-neutral recommendations backed by explainable AI
Safety: Built-in validation prevents costly compatibility errors
Proof Points:
"12 pilot customers avoided 2,000+ tons CO2e in first year"
"95% recommendation accuracy validated by user feedback"
"Average payback period: 2.3 years"
Content Marketing (Inbound Lead Gen)
Blog/SEO (Target: 5,000 monthly organic visits by Q4)
Topics:
"How to Calculate Scope 2 Emissions from Equipment Upgrades" (Sustainability Directors)
"Valve Efficiency: 15% Energy Savings Guide" (Facilities Managers)
"Total Cost of Ownership: Industrial Equipment Calculator" (Procurement)
"AI in Maintenance: Complete Guide" (Technical audience)
Cadence: 2 posts per week (Q2+)
Case Studies (3 by Q3)
Format: Problem → Solution → Quantified Outcomes
Example:
Chemical Manufacturer Reduces CO2e by 1,200 Tons Annually with AI-Powered Retrofits
Challenge: 500+ aging valves, no visibility into efficiency
Solution: Retrofit-Recommender identified top 50 retrofit opportunities
Results: $180K annual savings, 1,200 tons CO2e avoided, 2.1-year payback
Distribution: Website, sales collateral, PR, LinkedIn
Webinars (Monthly, starting Q2)
Topics:
"AI for Industrial Sustainability: Getting Started"
"How to Build a Data-Driven Decarbonization Plan"
"Retrofit ROI: Financial Justification Workshop"
Goal: 100+ attendees per webinar, 20% conversion to trial
Thought Leadership
Speaking: Present at ACEEE, Industrial Energy Technology Conference, CDP Forums
Whitepapers: "The State of Industrial Decarbonization 2026" (research report)
Podcast: Guest appearances on sustainability/manufacturing podcasts
Paid Marketing (Later Stage, Q3+)
LinkedIn Ads (B2B focus)
Target: Sustainability Directors, VPs Operations, Facilities Managers at Fortune 2000
Budget: $10K/month
Goal: 500 MQLs (Marketing Qualified Leads) per quarter
Google Ads (Intent-based)
Keywords: "equipment retrofit calculator", "industrial CO2 reduction", "maintenance ROI tool"
Budget: $5K/month
Goal: 200 freemium sign-ups per month
Events & Trade Shows
Tier 1 Events (Must Attend)
ACEEE Summer Study (August): Energy efficiency focus
Industrial Energy Technology Conference (May): Maintenance + sustainability
Greenbuild (November): Commercial buildings
Goal: 50 qualified leads per event, 3 pilot customers
Tier 2 Events (Sponsor/Attend)
Regional manufacturing associations
Industry-specific (Food Processing Expo, Chem Show)
Customer Acquisition Strategy
Phase 1: Design Partners (Q1 2026) ✅
Goal: 3 design partners for product validation
Criteria:
Willing to provide feedback weekly
500+ assets
Sustainability commitment (SBTi or similar)
Pilot fee: $5K (discounted from $10K)
Status: 2 signed, 1 in negotiation
Phase 2: Early Adopters (Q2 2026)
Goal: 10 paying customers
Targeting:
Expand from design partner referrals
Outbound to companies with recent sustainability announcements
Target industries: Chemical, automotive, food processing
Offer: 50% discount for first year ($15K vs. $30K) in exchange for case study participation
Phase 3: Scale (Q3-Q4 2026)
Goal: 50 paying customers
Tactics:
Launch freemium tier (PLG motion)
Ramp up direct sales team
Partner channel activates
Content marketing generates inbound leads
Success Metrics (12-Month)
Revenue Metrics
ARR: $2M
MRR Growth: 15% month-over-month (Q2-Q4)
Avg Deal Size: $40K
Customer Count: 50 paying customers
Efficiency Metrics
CAC (Customer Acquisition Cost): <$5K blended
LTV:CAC Ratio: >5:1
Sales Cycle: <6 months (enterprise), <3 months (mid-market)
Win Rate: >20% (demo to close)
Product Metrics
Freemium Sign-ups: 1,000+
Free-to-Paid Conversion: 10%
NPS: >45
Logo Retention: >90%
Impact Metrics
CO2e Avoided: 10,000 tons (cumulative across customers)
Recommendations Generated: 5,000+
Implementation Rate: 25%
Launch Plan (Q2 2026)
Pre-Launch (Weeks 1-4)
Finalize pricing & packaging
Build sales deck + demo environment
Create 3 case studies (from design partners)
Launch website v2.0 (professional branding)
Set up marketing automation (HubSpot/Salesforce)
Launch Week (Week 5)
Press release + industry media outreach
LinkedIn announcement (founder + company page)
Email to 500+ waitlist contacts
Launch webinar: "Introducing Retrofit-Recommender"
Activate freemium tier
Post-Launch (Weeks 6-12)
Weekly sales pipeline review
Bi-weekly marketing performance review
Monthly NPS survey
Quarterly business review with customers
Budget (12-Month)
Personnel ($800K)
VP Sales: $180K + $90K OTE
2 AEs: $120K + $60K OTE each
Inside Sales Manager: $90K + $45K OTE
2 SDRs: $60K + $30K OTE each
Customer Success: $80K
Marketing ($250K)

Events/Trade Shows: $75K

Total GTM Budget: $1.15M (Year 1)

Siemens Industrial Edge

Siemens hardware lock-in, no vendor-neutral recommendations
Vendor-neutral, 3x catalog breadth
Augury (Predictive Maintenance)
Real-time monitoring, failure prediction
Tells "when" but not "what to do", no sustainability ROI
Actionable recommendations + CO2e impact
Indirect Competitors
Sustainability consultants (Deloitte, EY, local firms): High-touch, expensive ($150-$300/hr), not scalable → We automate 80% of their work
Manual processes (Excel, tribal knowledge): Free but error-prone, slow, no audit trail → We provide instant, accurate, auditable analysis
Competitive Moats (Defensibility)
Data Network Effect: Every implementation improves recommendation accuracy (outcome feedback loop)
Domain Expertise: Proprietary knowledge base built from 100+ interviews + industry best practices
Sustainability IP: Patent-pending lifecycle carbon calculation methodology
Ecosystem Integrations: First-mover advantage with CMMS (Maximo, SAP) and carbon platforms (Watershed, Persefoni)

Marketing Strategy
Positioning Statement
For industrial facilities with sustainability commitments,
Retrofit-Recommender is an AI-powered platform
That transforms equipment retrofit decisions into quantified sustainability outcomes.
Unlike generic EAM software or expensive consultants,
Our product delivers instant, data-driven recommendations with CO2e impact and business case generation—reducing decision cycles from months to days.
Messaging Framework
Headline: Accelerate Industrial Decarbonization with AI-Powered Retrofit Recommendations
Key Messages:
Speed: Reduce retrofit decision time from 3 months to 3 days
Impact: Quantify CO2e reductions for sustainability reporting (CDP, TCFD)
ROI: Generate instant business cases with TCO, payback, and savings projections
Trust: Vendor-neutral recommendations backed by explainable AI
Safety: Built-in validation prevents costly compatibility errors
Proof Points:
"12 pilot customers avoided 2,000+ tons CO2e in first year"
"95% recommendation accuracy validated by user feedback"
"Average payback period: 2.3 years"
Content Marketing (Inbound Lead Gen)
Blog/SEO (Target: 5,000 monthly organic visits by Q4)
Topics:
"How to Calculate Scope 2 Emissions from Equipment Upgrades" (Sustainability Directors)
"Valve Efficiency: 15% Energy Savings Guide" (Facilities Managers)
"Total Cost of Ownership: Industrial Equipment Calculator" (Procurement)
"AI in Maintenance: Complete Guide" (Technical audience)
Cadence: 2 posts per week (Q2+)
Case Studies (3 by Q3)
Format: Problem → Solution → Quantified Outcomes
Example:
Chemical Manufacturer Reduces CO2e by 1,200 Tons Annually with AI-Powered Retrofits
Challenge: 500+ aging valves, no visibility into efficiency
Solution: Retrofit-Recommender identified top 50 retrofit opportunities
Results: $180K annual savings, 1,200 tons CO2e avoided, 2.1-year payback
Distribution: Website, sales collateral, PR, LinkedIn
Webinars (Monthly, starting Q2)
Topics:
"AI for Industrial Sustainability: Getting Started"
"How to Build a Data-Driven Decarbonization Plan"
"Retrofit ROI: Financial Justification Workshop"
Goal: 100+ attendees per webinar, 20% conversion to trial
Thought Leadership
Speaking: Present at ACEEE, Industrial Energy Technology Conference, CDP Forums
Whitepapers: "The State of Industrial Decarbonization 2026" (research report)
Podcast: Guest appearances on sustainability/manufacturing podcasts

Goal: 500 MQLs (Marketing Qualified Leads) per quarter
